_doc: Wire-format contract between Bedrock_Streaming_Handler_Staging/analytics_writer.js and Master_Function_Staging/analytics_writer.py. Both writers must emit identical UpdateExpression / ExpressionAttribute* / ConditionExpression for each fixture. The same file is duplicated in Master_Function_Staging/. CI runs both side-by-side against this fixture. See v7 plan §'PR A' lines 244-261 and §'PR A2' line 278.

redact_pii_cases._doc: 50-char truncation boundary. Truncation happens after redaction at the call site (analytics_writer), not inside redactPII itself.
redact_pii_cases.input: This question is exactly fifty-one characters long!!
redact_pii_cases.expected_redacted: This question is exactly fifty-one characters long!!
redact_pii_cases.expected_truncated_50: This question is exactly fifty-one characters long

fixtures.name: MESSAGE_SENT_initial
fixtures._doc: First user message in a brand-new session. attribute_not_exists branch of ConditionExpression hits.

expected.UpdateExpression: SET ended_at = :ended_at, session_id = if_not_exists(session_id, :session_id), tenant_id = if_not_exists(tenant_id, :tenant_id), started_at = if_not_exists(started_at, :started_at), #ttl = :ttl, last_request_id_message_sent = :request_id, first_question = if_not_exists(first_question, :first_question) ADD message_count :one, user_message_count :one

fixtures.name: MESSAGE_SENT_no_first_question
fixtures._doc: MESSAGE_SENT with empty first_question payload — first_question SET clause is omitted entirely.

expected.UpdateExpression: SET ended_at = :ended_at, session_id = if_not_exists(session_id, :session_id), tenant_id = if_not_exists(tenant_id, :tenant_id), started_at = if_not_exists(started_at, :started_at), #ttl = :ttl, last_request_id_message_sent = :request_id ADD message_count :one, user_message_count :one

SET ended_at = :ended_at, session_id = if_not_exists(session_id, :session_id), tenant_id = if_not_exists(tenant_id, :tenant_id), started_at = if_not_exists(started_at, :started_at), #ttl = :ttl, last_request_id_message_received = :request_id ADD message_count :one, bot_message_count :one, total_response_time_ms :response_time, response_count :one

SET ended_at = :ended_at, session_id = if_not_exists(session_id, :session_id), tenant_id = if_not_exists(tenant_id, :tenant_id), started_at = if_not_exists(started_at, :started_at), #ttl = :ttl, last_request_id_message_received = :request_id ADD message_count :one, bot_message_count :one

SET ended_at = :ended_at, session_id = if_not_exists(session_id, :session_id), tenant_id = if_not_exists(tenant_id, :tenant_id), started_at = if_not_exists(started_at, :started_at), #ttl = :ttl, #outcome = :outcome, last_request_id_form_completed = :request_id, form_id = :form_id

fixtures.name: validation_invalid_session_id
fixtures._doc: session_id contains a space — regex rejects. Writer returns false; emits log with reason from REASON_ENUM. NO UpdateItem call should be made.

fixtures.name: forward_compat_old_shape_row
fixtures._doc: Per CLAUDE.md schema discipline. The writer is ALWAYS appending new fields via if_not_exists / SET / ADD — old rows missing new fields must not crash the writer. This fixture is a contract sanity check that the UpdateExpression uses no attribute reads that would fail on a row missing the new last_request_id_* / response_count / total_response_time_ms fields.

expected._invariant: ConditionExpression uses attribute_not_exists OR <> — both branches succeed against an old-shape row that lacks last_request_id_message_sent.
